cases.claim_boundary: This is a prepared v2.4-style diagnostic opponent for fairness analysis. It is RT-core accelerated, but it does not use the v2.5 typed hit-stream handoff and does not authorize public speedup or true-zero-copy wording.

This prepared v2.5 path measures steady-state query/handoff/continuation after app-owned workload, payload, and scene preparation. It does not authorize true zero-copy, whole-app speedup, or RayDB reproduction claims.

neutral_buffer_handoff_summary.claim_boundary: Current hit-stream continuation metadata is neutral-buffer-aware. Native device-column output may be hardware-proven independently, but true zero-copy and public speedup claims remain unauthorized.

torch_carrier_adapter.claim_boundary: This explains the torch/Triton carrier bridge for hit-stream continuation. Raw CUDA-array columns are only no-copy candidates until pod evidence proves same-pointer DLPack/CUDA-array-interface adaptation without a host stage.

torch_carrier_execution.claim_boundary: Pointer equality is runtime evidence for the adapter only. It does not authorize true zero-copy or speedup claims without accepted pod review.
